Large-system NRE candidate-screening + exact-rerank experiment
date=2026-07-02
device=cuda
No retraining; frozen checkpoints and frozen voltage libraries are used.
Exact rerank@M means: rank all candidates by NRE, keep top M, then apply the same exact-reference voltage likelihood only inside that candidate set.

system,seed,M,n,n_candidates,candidate_fraction,nre_top1,exact_full_top1,truth_in_nre_topM,exact_winner_in_nre_topM,rerank_top1,rerank_vs_exact_full,nre_exact_agree,nre_mass_topM,nre_ms,exact_full_ms

300bus_IPC_miss30,50,3,0.2687,0.0052,0.4320,0.9989,0.9991,0.4313,0.0005,0.9991,0.2500
300bus_IPC_miss30,100,3,0.2687,0.0052,0.4320,1.0000,1.0000,0.4320,0.0000,1.0000,0.5000

Interpretation rule
For ordinary or moderate missingness, use NRE as the fast posterior engine.
For severe missingness or large-system stress, use NRE as the fast candidate-screening stage and apply exact/high-fidelity likelihood reranking to the retained credible set.
This preserves the fast amortized interface while making the severe-missing boundary evidence-backed rather than text-only.
elapsed_sec=1.7
